Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: storage boxes [300200342], storage containers [300197582], containers by function or context [300197200], containers (receptacles) [300197197], Containers (hierarchy name) [300045611], Furnishings and Equipment (hierarchy name) [300264551], Objects Facet [300264092]

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Boxes for holding matches; used especially with reference to small, pocket-sized, rectangular boxes. They can range from boxes made of metal and often having rounded corners and ornate decorations, made either with a hinged lid or a sliding drawerlike compartment, to a simple, small rectangular box of cardboard comprising a sliding compartment, usually with a roughened strip on the side for striking matches. Although these boxes may be called "pocket match safes," use "match safes" for larger, often wall-mounted boxes for holding matches.

Cajas para guardar cerillas; término usado especialmente con referencia a cajas pequeñas, de tamaño bolsillo, rectangulares de metal, a menudo con esquinas redondeadas y decoraciones ornamentales, hechas con tapas de bisagras o con un compartimento tipo cajón deslizable.

Doosjes voor het bevatten van lucifers. Wordt vooral gebruikt met betrekking tot kleine, metalen, rechthoekige dozen in zakformaat met afgeronde hoeken en drukke versieringen, hetzij gemaakt met een scharnierend deksel of met een glijdend vakje, als een lade.
一種用來盛裝火柴的盒子，尤指可收在口袋中的矩形小盒子；有些以金屬製成，多具圓角且裝飾華麗，附有以鉸鍊連接的蓋子或如抽屜可拉出的小隔間；有些則是以厚紙板製成的矩形小盒，形式簡單，有一個可以拉開的隔間，側邊通常有劃火柴用的粗糙條狀區塊。這些皆可稱作「口袋型火柴匣」，而掛在牆上盛放火柴用的大型容器則多稱為「火柴匣」。

Source.Source_ID: 2000046682/Random House Dictionary of the English Language (1987)
2000106166/Nomenclature for Museum Cataloging / Nomenclature pour le catalogage translation project (2016-)

Term_Source.Page: suggested at 6/92 Decorative Arts review as a lead-in to matchboxes
